# BasedAgents — full

> Scoped, revocable API keys for AI coding agents, plus a public registry that
> gives every agent a permanent cryptographic identity. This file mirrors the
> agent contract and docs index; the canonical machine-readable source is
> https://basedagents.ai/.well-known/agent.json and https://basedagents.ai/docs/agents.

## What this is

Keyring gives coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) their own scoped API
keys instead of your .env. Secrets live in an encrypted vault on the human's
machine; agents receive short in-memory leases, never raw long-lived keys.
Every key is bound to a cryptographic identity registered in the public
BasedAgents registry (Ed25519 keypair + proof-of-work → a permanent ag_ ID).
Humans claim their agents by email; the first approval mints a passkey that
signs every grant. Revoke (Disconnect) and the kill switch work on every plan.

## Agent flow (register → claim)

1. Register your identity.
   - CLI: `npx basedagents register`
   - API:
     a. POST https://api.basedagents.ai/v1/register/init {"public_key":"<base58_ed25519_pubkey>"}
        → { challenge_id, challenge, difficulty, expires_at }
     b. Solve proof-of-work: find a 4-byte nonce N such that
        sha256(pubkey_bytes || N_big_endian) has >= difficulty leading zero bits.
        Submit N as an 8-char zero-padded hex string (e.g. "006489f8").
     c. Sign the challenge: sign TextEncoder.encode(challenge) — the base64
        challenge string as raw UTF-8 bytes. Do NOT base64-decode it first.
     d. POST https://api.basedagents.ai/v1/register/complete
        {challenge_id, public_key, nonce, signature, profile:{name, description, capabilities, protocols}}
        → your permanent agent id: ag_<base58_pubkey>

2. Get your keys managed.
   - `npx basedagents keyring init` (equivalent alias: `npx @basedagents/keyring init`)
     installs an MCP server and creates a local encrypted vault + agent keypair.
     Tools: keyring_run, keyring_render, keyring_list, keyring_request, invite_owner
     (keyring_lease is off unless the grant sets unsafe_value_release).

3. Put a human in charge.
   - Call invite_owner("human@example.com"). They get an email
     ("An agent named <you> wants you as its owner") linking to
     https://app.basedagents.ai/start.
   - CLAIM-PENDING IS A HARD GATE: until the human clicks the magic link, your
     account holds authority over nothing. You may register and keyring_request,
     but no credential can be stored or leased. Invites expire in 72h and are
     rate-limited per agent id.

## After the human claims

- keyring_request(provider, scope?, note?) — the human approves with a passkey
  signature bound to the exact grant; their daemon re-verifies before sealing.
- keyring_lease(ref, context?, ttl_seconds?) — receive a short-lived, in-memory
  copy; leases expire on their own; every use is signed into a local log.

## AgentSig auth

- Header: Authorization: AgentSig <base58_pubkey>:<base64_ed25519_signature>
- Also: X-Timestamp: <unix_seconds>
- Signed message: <METHOD>:<path>:<timestamp>:<sha256_hex_of_body>

## Links

- Manifest: https://basedagents.ai/.well-known/agent.json
- Agent docs: https://basedagents.ai/docs/agents
- Keyring: https://basedagents.ai/keyring
- Registry: https://basedagents.ai/registry
- Get started (human): https://app.basedagents.ai/start
- API docs: https://api.basedagents.ai/docs
- Source: https://github.com/maxfain/basedagents
